summary: Full overhaul of benchmark scoring, scenario design, and condition design across 9 implementation steps
context: Analysis of 4 complete benchmark runs showed conditions are within 1.8 points of each other (66.4-68.2 composite avg). Investigation revealed this flatness is caused by broken metrics (50% of context-recovery composite always 0), leaky scenario design (answers given in prompts, decisions embedded in code), and an overly aggressive baseline (strips project docs).
rationale: Fix scoring bugs first (P1) to get valid measurement, then fix scenario design (P2) to create genuine coordination needs, then fix conditions (P3) to properly isolate coordination value. Both scorer fixes AND Agent A timeout reduction for context-recovery (user chose "both"). Full overhaul scope (user chose P1+P2+P3).

alternatives.reason_rejected: User chose full overhaul — scoring fixes alone won't create genuine coordination needs in scenarios

alternatives.option: Fix scorer only for context-recovery (no timeout change)
alternatives.reason_rejected: User chose both — scorer fix handles edge cases while shorter timeout ensures incomplete work reliably
